extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked inside final km on lumpy circuit finish, held off BETANCUR and late-charging FRANK/COSTA to take both stage and yellow.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint burned matches attacking at 900m to go but lacked final sprint speed, losing to fresher finishers.
BUCHMANN rode aggressively for intermediate sprint points despite starting 15 seconds down, signaling GC bonus ambitions early.
ROGLIČ positioned in select front group through five second-category climbs, conserving energy while rivals attacked before final km strike.
BETANCUR stayed on THOMAS wheel rather than countering, tactical passivity costing him stage when ROGLIČ launched final acceleration.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: won sprint from select group after extended lumpy stage despite claiming rust from long Tirreno layoff
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: initiated final attack at 900m but faded to fourth, first race since Tour de France win showing lack of racing sharpness
rider_signals.BETANCUR Carlos: neutral: matched THOMAS attack but sat on wheel rather than countering, settled for second
rider_signals.FRANK Mathias: positive: late sprint surge nearly caught leaders despite not being in initial attacking group
rider_signals.COSTA Rui: positive: closed gap in final meters with strong sprint, demonstrated better finishing speed than established GC riders
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: aggressive early for bonus seconds despite deficit, showing willingness to spend energy for marginal gains
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: neutral: mentioned as very strong but no attacking evidence shown, passive approach in select group
